---
title: "Model Distributions"
author: "Marius 't Hart"
date: "2026-07-16"
output:
  word_document: default
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
source('R/model_sims.R')
```

## Model Distributions

First we have the simulations for all 4 cases: for both aiming and adaptation we run the stepfunction and exponential style models.

```{r save_simulations, eval=FALSE}
saveSimulations()
```

This stores the parameters as well, but not the NLL. Anyways, now we can plot them.

Here are the data and model distributions for adaptation:

```{r adapt_distr_plots, fig.width=8, fig.height=10}
plotDataAndFits(signal='adapt')
```


And here are the data and model distributions for aiming:

```{r aiming_distr_plots, fig.width=8, fig.height=10}
plotDataAndFits(signal='aiming')
```
